auc: 0.894 (aided, 95% Bootstrap CI: 0.879, 0.909); 0.836 (unaided, 95% Bootstrap CI: 0.816, 0.856); Standalone overall: 0.976 (95% Bootstrap CI: 0.975, 0.976)
intended_use: Chest-CAD is a computer-assisted detection (CADe) software device that analyzes chest radiograph studies using machine learning techniques to identify, categorize, and highlight suspicious regions of
intended_use_full: Chest-CAD is a computer-assisted detection (CADe) software device that analyzes chest radiograph studies using machine learning techniques to identify, categorize, and highlight suspicious regions of interest (ROI). Any suspicious ROI identified by Chest-CAD is assigned to one of the following categories: Cardiac, Mediastinum/Hila, Lungs, Pleura, Bones, Soft Tissues, Hardware, or Other. The device is intended for use as a concurrent reading aid for physicians. Chest-CAD is indicated for adults only.
target_condition: Suspicious regions of interest in chest radiographs
ground_truth: Binary labels assigned by a panel of U.S. board-certified radiologists indicating presence or absence of suspicious ROI for each Chest-CAD category
other_metrics: Standalone testing: sensitivity 0.908 (95% Wilson's CI: 0.905, 0.911); specificity 0.887 (95% Wilson's CI: 0.885, 0.889). Category-specific AUCs ranged from 0.921 (Mediastinum/Hila) to 0.994 (Hardware). Category-specific sensitivity ranged from 0.854 (Bones) to 0.967 (Hardware). Category-specific specificity ranged from 0.830 (Mediastinum/Hila) to 0.960 (Hardware).
